_comment: ADC clock correction database for USB microphones used with cinepi-raw.
Some USB audio devices run their internal ADC clock slightly off the nominal
sample rate. A mic that captures 47 946 Hz instead of the declared 48 000 Hz
will produce a WAV that is ~1 130 ppm too short, causing audio to drift ahead
of video by roughly 10 frames over a 6-minute take.
This file maps USB mic names to their measured clock offset in parts-per-million
(ppm). Cinemate looks up the active mic at launch and passes the offset to
cinepi-raw, which resamples the finished WAV after each take to correct the
duration without touching the BWF timecode anchor.
Correction is only applied when BOTH conditions are met:
1. audio.clock_correction.enabled is true in src/settings.json
2. The connected mic matches an entry in this file
HOW TO FIND THE DEVICE NAME
Run the following command on the Pi:
arecord -l
Look for a line like:
card 2: VideoMicGOII [RODE VideoMic GO II], device 0: USB Audio [USB Audio]
The value inside the square brackets after the colon is the card name.
Use that string (or any substring of it) as the key below.
Matching is case-insensitive, and the first match found wins.
HOW TO MEASURE PPM FOR A NEW DEVICE
1. Make sure Phase 1 (SCHED_FIFO) and Phase 2 (dsnoop settle) fixes are in
place so storage-driven xruns are not contaminating the measurement.
2. Record a 6-minute take under autostart (not a manual cinemate shell).
Give three audible claps: one ~2 s in, one at ~3:00, one ~2 s before stop.
3. After the take, confirm zero 'Inserted silent frame' lines in the log:
journalctl -u cinemate-autostart --since '15 min ago' | grep 'silent frame'
If silent frames appear, xruns are still contributing to drift — fix those
first before measuring ADC clock offset.
4. Align audio and video in an NLE using the BWF timecode ('source audio-start').
5. Measure the frame offset at each clap relative to the video clap.
6. Calculate ppm from start-clap to end-clap:
ppm = (delta_frames / (delta_seconds * fps)) * 1 000 000
Example: 10 frames of drift over 356 s at 25 fps
7. Run two takes and average the results for a stable value.
HOW TO ADD A NEW DEVICE
Add an entry to the 'devices' object below:
"Device Name Substring": {
"ppm": <measured_integer_value>,
"note": "optional: conditions, date, take count used for measurement"
Set audio.clock_correction.enabled to true in src/settings.json to activate.

RØDE VideoMic NTG.note: Measured 2026-06-02, 4K NVMe exFAT, two 6-minute takes under cinemate-autostart. Consistent 1117–1145 ppm across both takes, zero xruns. ADC runs at ~47 946 Hz instead of 48 000 Hz nominal.
